rationale: Both hit the latent gap directly and concretely: X pinpoints 5 existing tests that call sleep(level=2)+wake_up() and will now raise (exact reproduction of the #4905 break), while Y independently diagnoses the root cause (default level=2 bricks the common sleep/wake path) and separately flags that the new guard logic isn't exercised in standard CI. X is slightly more surgical and evidence-heavy (specific test names/lines), giving it an edge in precision, but Y has broader recall — it touches all three ground-truth threads (regression-test adequacy, the CuMemTag enum, and an unrelated-CI-failure caveat mirroring Flink-ddd's actual comment) versus X's near-total silence on CI. Both are highly actionable with concrete file/line fixes; Y's structured findings table and conditioned verdict edge out X's flatter list.
